I can't believe what's happening. I looked in the back and I started counting I was counting the people my 2004 6 8 10 I was doing the math. I was like that guy just made $50,000 in one 90 minute presentation. Again, I spent $2,000 to be there. He the event promoter had made over a million dollars selling selling things at this event The way that those seminars typically work is the person who prons it puts on the event brings in speakers And then the speaker sells their product the speaker gets half the money and the event promoter gets half the money So there's over a million dollars made of this event and 500,000 went to the dude who was running the event and the other $500,000 went to these speakers and I remember sitting there thinking I got to learn how to be a speaker I think this is a crazy and And at the time and that's something I just probably won't believe this but I was a super super shy kid In fact in my my own personal like my real life. I'm a very shy person But I learned how to be a speaker and learn how to present the right way because I wanted to make money Okay, I'm a capitalist just like all of us right. I want to make money I saw these guys are making crazy money doing art hardly anything I want to I want to do it and so I started trying to go out there and learn how to become a public speaker And what was interesting is the the very first Event I went to after that I was watch as wanted to see some other people do it so I can kind of get the gist of how it worked and I vividly remember looking at these speakers and and as they were teaching stuff I'd say man, I can teach better stuff than this my stories are better my my content's better I like these guys aren't even teaching that that that good of stuff. I remember being frustrated and so finally My very first chance I had to speak on stage I got all excited and what I went up there and I had all my content laid out I had like all my best stuff all my best ideas that I never had put in this presentation And I wanted to wow the audience and I went there and I shared with them so much stuff way more than anybody else had ever Had I ever seen give it an event and I was like these guys are gonna love me so much They are gonna come and buy my stuff and I just blew their minds with with all this just awesome this and guess what happened nothing Nobody moved I closed at the end and I sold my product Nobody went to the back of the room and it was embarrassing I literally standing at the stage watch nobody pop up Nobody moved and I then quietly walked to the back of the audience and people kind of shuffled out and left for lunch And it was the most awkward worst feeling I've ever felt in my life. I was so embarrassed I literally walked out of that room walked to my hotel room and I shut the door and for three days the rest of it I sat in the room. I was too embarrassed to come back down. I remember sitting there And I was so frustrated and so angry and part of me just wanted to quit You know, I said it doesn't make sense. It's not fair like I I paid my dues. I've done this. I've you know, I've I've You know, I the stuff I taught was amazing. Why in the world are these people buying it? I was so just frustrated and just heartened and And luckily for me, I decided not to quit I decided I'm gonna you know, I'm gonna try again. All right Okay, so let me let me go through this so perfect webinar so the webinar there's basically There's basically three sections of the perfect webinar the first is your introduction second is the content section and Then the third is your clothes Okay, and this is kind of how I how I structure these all right Okay, so I'll begin with the The introduction now the introduction is is probably the easiest part, but the biggest key To the introduction you guys is you can't be boring Okay, and people typically can't see if you're doing if you're speaking from stage Then people will see you as you as you use the script Okay, and this is the same script I use when I'm speaking from stage to sell as they do on a webinar But on a webinar people can't see you okay So the biggest thing is like your tonality in your voice is what keys people around if you talk like this and you're kind of quiet Nobody will buy your product. Okay, if you look at me right now, I'm literally sitting on the edge of my seat I have my hands in front of me and I'm moving them around and I my voice goes up really really high and it goes down And I have to have tonality have to be exciting have to get people engaged if you watch my webinars you notice that what's interesting is I Learned this first time when I was I we did an infomercial a little while ago and for the infomercial I remember The guy the infomercial producer told me said look Russell if you talk like you normally do on TV You're gonna sound like this is if you talk super excited and kind of hype it over the top You're gonna sound normal and it's the same on a webinar You have to come with more energy than you do in your real world Otherwise people are gonna zone out and they're gonna fall asleep. Okay, so I'm like at the level 10 right now Just talking to you guys. I'm not even selling you something right now, but if I was it's even more important Okay, so starting with a lot of energy and being exciting usually about a minute or two before the webinar starts I like getting on and just saying hey everyone. This is the webinar. We're gonna be talking about high ticket secrets You're just gonna be awesome. We're about two minutes away from getting started. Hey everyone. This is Russell We'll be you know, I keep looping that thing because people are always calling in and so you can't just like hey guys This is Russell get started in a minute Hey guys, this Russell get started in 30 seconds. They get to have constant energy So I'm always I just keep repeating myself like like a like a broken loop over and over again It keeps the energy up it keeps people excited and whenever they're dialing in and answering They're hearing me talk and they're hearing that excitement and they feel like they're at something cool Okay, get some excited and usually I'll even run that over maybe three or four minutes past when the webinar supposed to start I think hey guys Webinars, you know the webinar officially started about a minute ago, but I'm not gonna start because people are still logging I'm gonna give me one or two more minutes, but I want you to let you guys know you're in the right spot I'm about to show you exactly how you can instantly increase your high ticket sales blah blah blah blah blah right and I keep recapping that till about two or three minutes in and now I'm gonna start Okay, so that's key search kind of my intro now. I lead initially with a bold promise Okay, this bold promise is gonna tie back here to what I call the one thing with the bold promise is kind of like I call it a ruler, okay It's like someone's everyone's measuring you all the time, right? And if I come on a webinar I start teaching they're gonna measure me versus every other webinar They've ever been on in the past and they've been on people that are awesome on webinars. That's what they're gonna judge me against That's what the ruler is that they're gonna say was that a good webinar or bad It's all gonna be based on the ruler that they're using Okay, and so that I don't want to have them judge me based on just my presentation skills alone because again I'm gonna talk too fast. I'm gonna see a lot of ums. I'm gonna slur things I want to judge. I want them to judge me based on what I'm promising. So I'm saying this is my big promise So for me, here's my big promise. I said hey guys I'm gonna show you how to instantly add high ticket sales to your sales funnel without you ever personally talking on the phone to anyone Ever okay at the end this webinar I'm gonna you're gonna learn exactly how to do that and it's sold in this webinar will be a benefit success Okay, boom big huge promise and that's not what they're judging it on They're judging the entire webinar based on if this this promise. I just made them if it's true Okay, then if they're like yeah Russell taught me taught me exactly how to build a sales funnel out personally talking on the phone And whenever boom, okay, that's the ruler. They're judging me again. So it's perfect Okay, next I'm gonna hook them to the end so again my goal is if you stay to the end of this webinar I'm gonna do something really really cool Okay, so what I did for for for this audience I said, you know we're talking about calls and I said like if you guys stay till the end I'm actually gonna I'm gonna open my laptop put on my webcam and walk in you can actually see my call center You guys want to see that people are going crazy. They're so excited. Okay, you only get that if you stay till the end Okay, now we've hooked them Okay, cuz they're the first four or five minutes webinars when like 90% of your people leave those that are gonna leave You can pass that first little hump. They're gonna stay the entire 90 minutes Okay, so you got to hook them fast so you can hook them with a bonus with the prize with free something with you know I've seen people give away iPads and give away Phones and just some kind of hook you have to have okay, so it could be anything just something to hook them to the end Okay, and then I start commanding attention usually I say something like hey guys if you got your cell phone out If you have Skype Facebook yet close it down. We need you I need your attention Okay, if you're not paying attention, none of this will help you at all So you need to make sure you're paying attention trying to command attention then I qualify myself So look this is why I actually know what I'm talking about Okay, and I share for this one. I should have pictured me sleeping just to be funny Kind of a pattern interrupt and then I came back to look I'm I don't talk about us a lot, but I'm the king of high-ticket sales I did back in sales for Dan Kennedy for Bill Glaser for Mike Phil saying for Ray and Kern plus a ton of other marketers We've done over eight figures of high-ticket sales There's nobody in the marketplace better than me at high-ticket sales and I'm gonna show you exactly how we do it boom I qualify myself. Okay, you guys gotta qualify yourself. Why is what you do? Why are you qualified to do this? Okay, why are you different than everybody else who's kind of teaching something like that? You got to qualify yourself and stick it out there so the people they kind of get that And then from there, I like to future pace and then say Well, here's sorry. There's some of these slides are still me qualifying. So here's my qualification I did a lot of things right and a bunch of things wrong after this for 10 years This is kind of what I figured out and this is kind of leading back to into my content I just got the hard way, but you're gonna have a chance to cheat again. Here's the hard way. I did it was really hard and frustrating Boom and then I this is where I switch to the future pacing Okay, I say look So future pacing as you know future pacing is I try to put them in a state where they can a visual They can visualize what will happen when they've accomplished this thing Okay, so if you ever heard me if you ever heard me say on a webinar or anything I'm like, you guys imagine what's gonna be like someday when your business is up and running and you're making your first thousand dollars Like would that be awesome? So I'm doing I'm doing future pacing. Okay, it's an help It's a concept from NLP where I'm trying to take you to a place in the future where you've already achieved your goals Okay, so if I can do that again, it's part of the intro. I'm like so right here I said look do you guys want an easy way that's gonna require you to never pick up a phone? Can you imagine that like making high ticket sales out every afternoon pick up the phone ever like what would you do with your life? What would you do different? Okay, what would in a month from now when you're doing that? Like what what's it gonna be like? What's your what's your spouse gonna say what your kids gonna think when I was in their dad's at home? And they're making twice as much money pretty exciting. Don't you think okay? Boom, I'm future pacing him. All right pushing him out into the future All right, then I kind of go back. This is just more again The script is the script is is a framework and I add things in between the just I want to right sometimes this one I just kind of bragged a little bit last 14 days or over six figures in high ticket products I didn't pick up the phone and then I like throwing in some kind of like FTC disclaimer like results are not typical This is not gonna work blah blah blah just to keep yourself kind of safe and now I shift over here to To the content part of the webinar, okay I want to step back one of the biggest problems that people make when they're creating a webinar or any sales piece for that matter It can be sales videos sells letters webinars everything is Because they're so in love with their product or their service and there's so many facets of it They they try to they try to like sell something at every single thing that their thing can do right all the different all the big All the big the big benefits of it in fact one of our inner circle members I was just reviewing his sales video and he's crushing They're doing I think it did a million bucks last year with their sales video But I said the biggest problem with your sales video right now The reason why you're converting it one or two percent versus five or six percent is because you're selling four or five different things People have to believe five different things you want to buy your product Okay, I learned this originally from Perry Belcher. He talked about he said that when he had sales letters that that tried to get people to believe In two or more things they always flopped He said he said if I had sales letters that just try to get somebody to believe one thing one core thing if I did that Then they were successful Okay, and I saw the exact same thing happen to me you guys I cannot tell you how many times all the webinars I've done when I try to like okay to get somebody to buy this They need to believe in this this and this so I structure where I'm trying to teach them all these things And you can't do it that way that'll destroy your sales. Okay, it's the way the reason why when I first did this it didn't work I get on stage. I'm gonna show you my seven best things at how I make money I show seven things and they're like wow I got a lot of value out of that but I got seven things I gotta go do now and it would never buy right so you have to figure out from From your entire presentation from everything you do. What's the one thing? Okay, what is that one thing that is is going to change and just make Like the one thing they got to have they have that one thing it'll all make sense Okay, if you look back here to my title this was my one thing Okay, one thing I learned from Dagen Smith. So let me tell you the stack is so typically when and I learned this From arm and more and I will give him 100% credit He's made me like literally the stack has made me over $10 million in direct sales I can tie back to this concept. I'm gonna teach you right now called the stack It was before I spoke my first time speaking Armins event and it was two weeks before we were a dad Kennedy event Armigot there he spoke to an audience with thousand people and he closed like I don't know It was insane like 30 or 40 or 50 percent of the audience they ran back and he did like I don't know It was just shy of a million dollars in sales by himself And I remember watching I just like that was the coolest thing ever and afterwards He came up to me with this huge stack of order forms in his hands He's like you want a bunch of order forms like this next week or two weeks when you speak in my event like yeah, man I totally do like what I got to do and he said I Got to teach us the process called the stack. He's like if you do it you'll get a big stack of order forms You don't do it. You're gonna not can make much money. It's like that's just how it works I'm like, all right. Well, how's it work? He said this is the big secret He said most of the presenters you watch the way they do their presentation. They'll say hey The first thing you're gonna get is this and then you're gonna get this and you're gonna get this He keeps walking through all the stuff they're gonna get and then the last slide that like this is bonus number 12 That's what you're gonna get and they go over They go over to the next slide like I'm gonna give you all this stuff for you know, $10,000 and then they start doing the price drop Now what happens? He says the only thing that the human mind remembers is the last thing they were showed so Right before he's like right before you show them this big price The only thing they remember is bonus number 10 or 12 Whatever your last thing is you showed and that bonus doesn't justify that price in their mind Then they're not gonna buy it. He said so I invented this thing called the stack is this how it works You can kind of see here. He says I start my presentation I stack like like the first component and I talk about it and then Actually, I'll show you guys how it works down here. So when I did when I did the stack first thing it is I show right here I said, let me talk about what you're gonna get. Okay first thing you can get is this So I do the first stack second thing you're gonna do is you're gonna get this and third thing you're gonna do is this Okay, if I was if I was clicking through these are animated fourth thing you're gonna do is this boom So this is the total value 8,000 bucks. So this is the core offer. Okay, so if any of you guys If any of you guys are Like if you have your core thing you're teaching somebody So because it's gonna be six modules or it's gonna be you know four modules teaching bubble That's what this was. This is my core my core offer, right? I'm a sub a two-step. I'm sure you have a sub a two-step funnel. I'll be sub mini call center I'm gonna give you the actual script for you. I'm gonna give you Robbie in a box. Boom. There's the core the core offer, right? Now I'm gonna start adding value. That's these little smiley face guys are I'm adding value, right? So I'm going to give you this bonus which is unlimited traffic boom. I'm gonna give you this bonus which is Our ads or interviews. Okay, so what that means is now I stack this that means you're gonna get I can recap The first thing I told them the second thing thing the third thing the fourth thing So I just I just reset that and I say plus you're gonna now get bonus number one Which is unlimited traffic, which is the $1,997 value plus you get bonus number two Which is hiring commissions and more for nine nine hundred ninety seven dollars. So now the total values package is eleven thousand nine hundred ninety eight dollars He has probably seen me do this before right? There's few other presenters. Okay, it's kind of cheesy, right? They go through I start doing some these other closes and other closes other closes And I come back and I add another bonus, okay? I'm gonna give you this console a 30-minute console with Robbie, which is the two thousand dollar value they come back Boom I get the guarantee guarantee boom and I stack it again Which means you're gonna get the two-step funnel you're gonna get this mini call center value to two thousand you're gonna and literally you guys by The time you've done the stack like five or six times You're gonna be so embarrassed and I remember first time I did this at a seminar people were laughing and and it got kind of like ridiculous But guess what? Everybody knew exactly what the offer was and they bought like crazy So I don't not ever do this because it works so good. Okay, well number three. I'm gonna give you this I'm gonna give you this and then which means you're also gonna get this and you can get this and then like I just told you I'm gonna add in this bonus as well It's total value now is this price that awesome and I drop in some more closes more closes boom Recap again, so you're gonna stack it again, and then I give them the price and then they can buy it Okay, so that's kind of the the form of how the stack works. The total price is Somewhere boom Okay, the total price is $11,998 I show the big huge price now most people they do that and they get scared like oh, but don't worry guys I'm not gonna charge you that And what happens you just lost your credibility. I'm not gonna charge you that Ho ho ho and they move on that's like the worst thing That's like the worst thing Um, and this is again this attack I learned from from Dustin and Dave is as soon as I revealed a huge price Right. I show the $11,000 thing now. I need to make that price I need to justify that price in their head if I don't justify that price in their head Then they're not then then when I actually sell the product it's not gonna work I have to I have to get them mentally to believe that it's actually worth that And if I don't then I lost the sale Okay, so after I show this high ticket thing this now is where Um, this is where I actually add my if-all statements. Okay. I learned this from from Dave van Hoos So I I set the high price and I say look guys I'm not I'm not gonna charge $11,998 today. I could but I'm not going to but I want to ask you something And then this is where you add your if-all If all that this package did was I think so too Now if all this package did was give you two to three high ticket clients per month that would be worth it Yeah, I don't know about you, but I I twidled $12,000 for two or three, you know I'm not gonna give you two or three high ticket clients per month I'm not gonna give you two or three high ticket clients per month. That would be worth it Yeah, I don't know about you, but I I twidled $12,000 for two or three, you know $5 to $10,000 sales month all day long. This is one time investment You do it once you never have to pay again, but that knowledge the information everything you got will pay you forever Okay, so these if-all statements they solidify this price in their mind That makes sense you guys if falls And now I come back say but don't worry guys. I'm not gonna charge you that In fact, what I'm gonna do is and you start dropping the price Okay, I went a little bit further because I talked about about two or three clients What's that worth to him? Okay, so disposable income. This is where um, you you talk about The the fact that like like all of us have disposable income like most you guys You get your paycheck each month and a percentage that goes towards Towards movies and towards things like that And uh, and I'm not asking you guys to to go and go into depth for this at all What I'm trying to ask you guys to do is that some of that disposable income that you're gonna spend on the movies and And going out to dinner and all that kind of stuff instead of blowing it on something That's just gonna be there and then kind of fade away and you're gonna lose it You I I'm asking you to make an investment yourself because the money you put into this is going to turn into more money Okay, a lot of times like talking about I might talk about robert kiyosaki here Like you guys have read the read the book rich dad poor dad now in the book rich dad poor dad He talked about difference between assets and liabilities. He said that the poor people they buy liabilities Okay, they buy cars they buy they buy boats they buy these things you put money in and the money disappears Okay, um, what the rich people do is they put their money into investments that actually turn into more money So all I'm doing is ask you to divert some of your disposable income into an into an investment As opposed to something that's going to take money away from you. Okay, that's kind of how I use How I do the disposable income close. Okay money replenish this close this one is One of my my other favorite ones. So money replenishes basically um, the the concept is Is look every single month you get a paycheck in fact, I wonder if I where I put this one in here I Might be the find on the fly so as you can see though, I sprinkled these out throughout the stack And I'm not gonna find it real quick anyway money replenish this so the basis like look this is how it works Every two weeks you get paid a paycheck, right? And the cool thing about it is you you get that paycheck you go out you pay your bills You buy your you know your disposable income you spell it money and the money disappears, right? But what happens two weeks later? You get paid again that money replenishes and the cool thing about money is money always replenishes Okay, every two weeks it comes back again every two weeks comes back again. He's coming back in over and over and over again, right? Let me ask you a question Does that happen with your time too? Like when you when you uh, you miss your your daughter's soccer games. You're at the office like do you get that time back? No, it doesn't huh? It's not weird like money replenishes every single two weeks But but your time never comes back So i'm asking you to do is to take a take an asset take a resource that replenishes every two weeks is going to come back You're gonna get that money back every two weeks and Invest in something that's going to save you a ton of time because you can learn this if you can go do what I did You can go hire 60 sales people you can go and build your own call center You can build a sales team you can hire you can do all stuff But i'm trying to do with this package what i created for you guys I'm just trying to save you a bunch of time, okay? And because you can't get your time back i'm asking you to take money which is replenishable In two weeks from now you're gonna have that money back anyway. It doesn't even matter And do that to help to give you something that's going to save you this is going to save you the time Okay, so you show them how money replenishes and they're like, yeah, you're right. It's going to be back My time wow my time doesn't come back Okay, and you can learn this stuff on your own and then again this works in any market You can go learn how to how to how to lose weight on your own Man that time you waste Testing and trial that money that time doesn't come back your money's gonna be back in two weeks keeps coming back My clockwork, okay, and that's how it works even if you lose your job. Guess what the government's going to replenish it for you It keeps coming back. Okay, so take some of that money blow it on Blow it on my stuff instead and then it'll it'll be back in two weeks anyway. Okay. All right Uh next close is the the break old habits close. This one's awesome because um They're in the webinar. They're all fired up. They're excited like I want to ask you as a question So a lot of you guys You've probably been on these kind of webinars before in the past right you get excited you get fired up and what happens the next day Probably nothing for most of you guys Okay, it's sad because I just showed you guys the exact system I'm using right now to make a hundred thousand dollars a week you're gonna go back tomorrow not do anything Okay, the reason why is because old habits that are hard to break. Okay, you'll go back to your office next day And you're gonna be back at the exact same habit the only way you're gonna be successful is if we figure out a way to break That habit right now Okay, and the way you're gonna break that habit is by investing this course now I can come here and I can help you and then and give you all the tools you need to break that habit So you can become successful then tell you If you don't do that you can slide back to your exact habit Okay, everything we did for the last hour here will be completely useless because Because you're slip back to your old habit. So the last slide I said look guys if you still have questions, that's fine I understand that You know, I want you guys locking your spot right now So what you can do is if you have a question all you got to do is call our secure voicemail line at this number And then leave a detailed message with your full name your address your phone number and your credit card name number with expret with Expiration and don't worry. I'm not going to charge right now I just want to make sure you're actually serious not just calling to to to be a lookie loop if you're serious about this You just have a question give me everything including your credit card and I will not bill I swear to you I won't bill it But what happened as soon as we get that that information in either my me or my assistant will call you today or tomorrow And we will personally answer your question and then if you're okay with everything then we'll bill your credit card That's not fair People are like, ah, sure and all of a sudden you'll start seeing your your voice light up your voicemail So for this we set up a google voice number And people they log in and they they leave their message leave their phone number And we got a 25 percent increase in sales from adding that little thing in there from all those people who they're in they just got one question It's magic. Okay, so that's a really good one and the last one Um, this one's kind of a bigger thing Kind of a higher an overall hierarchy principle I learned this initially from a guy named ted thomas They call him the pied piper because when he speaks at like a robert kiyosaki event I've seen pictures of him He'll literally be walking around the room and he'll have a line of like 500 people all holding their credit card falling him behind him waving their credit cards and they'll fall into the back of the room and uh It's uh, it's pretty awesome. Anyway, they call him the pied piper because of that and I met him at an event He saw me speak and then afterwards he pulled me inside and he said rustle you did you did great You did sales were good. He said, you know, it's one thing he said when when you were uh When you were speaking so I was sitting in the back of the audience and you did a great job You know people signed up. They were excited, but I noticed nobody's heads were moving He said when you come to my audience and you watch me speak you said and you're sitting in the back of the room It looks like you're watching a wave so everybody's heads are nodding up and down the entire 90 minutes Okay, he said he said you know how I do that. I said no, how do you do? He said I do it by this little thing called trial closes You know all the trial closes is is something you I throw out there to get somebody to say yes. Does that make sense? Okay, I said does that make sense because that was a trial close He has no probably notice I say that a lot. Okay. I say isn't that awesome. You guys getting this Isn't this exciting like when you see how this all works and and everyone listening at home saying yeah That is exciting. That is cool. They start nodding their heads. Yes Okay, so the first time I learned about these trial closes I went back to an automated webinar I had and I literally went through and I rewatched the entire like three hour long webinar And I listened to every single spot. I could insert a trial close and I wrote this thing out and I had my brother record me Um, or I record myself saying each these old trial closes and I had my brother Who just does my video stuff insert those trial closes in my entire three hour webinar? and then we started running it and our um our profit per Per webinar registrant went from nine dollars and like 40 cents a person up to 16 dollars It was like almost we almost doubled our income just by adding in these trial closes. It's crazy Okay, now for me when I first started doing this what I would do is I would take all these trial closes And I'd write them down on note cards and I put them all over my desk and I was giving webinars I looked down I would say him I would get to a spot. I'd be like, isn't that awesome. Are you guys getting this? Okay, do you guys see how this would help you in your life? Can you imagine what it'd be like if you were doing this every single day? Okay, and I started saying that all over the all over the place and now it's a point where if you guys just watch this webinar I'm not even trying to close you by guarantee your head's been nodding I've been dropping trial closes every like 10 seconds on you guys It's just how I speak now, right? And it's something you guys will learn as well But trial closes are powerful and best place to add trial closes. Oh besides the entire presentation But anytime you share a success story or you share one of your big secrets Most people will share testimonial and then they move on can we share testimonial? You should drop five or six trial closes right afterwards So I just showed how Susie did this thing as she made she lost 30 pounds and then I'm gonna stop and be like Isn't that awesome? What happened? Can you imagine if you lost 30 pounds right now? Would that be awesome? What would what would what would that mean for you in your life if you lost 30 pounds?
